== Editing Cheatsheet (Visual Mode {plus} Clipboard)

*Leader* = `Space`

ŌåÆ Back to xref:editor/keybindings.adoc[main cheatsheet]

== Visual Mode Editing

[cols=",,",options="header",]
|===
|Keys |Mode |Action
|`++<++` |Visual |Outdent selection and reselect
|`++>++` |Visual |Indent selection and reselect
|`++<++Tab++>++` |Visual |Indent selection
|`++<++S-Tab++>++` |Visual |Outdent selection
|`++<++S-Down++>++` |Visual |Move selected block down
|`++<++S-Up++>++` |Visual |Move selected block up
|`++<++leader++>++p` |Visual |Paste without overwriting clipboard
|===

== System Clipboard

With `clipboard=unnamedplus` set, the default `y`, `d`, and `p` already
use the system clipboard on most platforms. The shortcuts below use the
explicit `"{plus}` register for unambiguous clipboard access (useful
when you need to be certain you are targeting the system clipboard, not
just the unnamed register).

[cols=",,",options="header",]
|===
|Keys |Mode |Action
|`++<++leader++>++y` |Normal / Visual |Yank to system clipboard
|`++<++leader++>++Y` |Normal |Yank line to system clipboard
|`++<++leader++>++d` |Normal / Visual |Cut (delete) to system clipboard
|`++<++leader++>++p` |Normal |Paste from system clipboard after cursor
|`++<++leader++>++P` |Normal |Paste from system clipboard before cursor
|===

=== Platform notes

[width="100%",cols="34%,33%,33%",options="header",]
|===
|Environment |Clipboard tool |Install needed?
|GUI Linux (X11) |`xclip` or `xsel` |Yes ŌĆö `sudo apt install xclip`

|GUI Linux (Wayland) |`wl-clipboard` |Yes ŌĆö
`sudo apt install wl-clipboard`

|WSL |`win32yank.exe` |Yes ŌĆö install on the *Windows* side
(Scoop/Chocolatey)

|SSH / TTY / console |OSC 52 (built-in) |No ŌĆö requires Neovim Ōēź 0.10 and
a supporting terminal

|Native Windows |`win32yank.exe` |Usually bundled with Neovim Windows
installer
|===

ŌåÆ *xref:editor/clipboard.adoc[Full setup and troubleshooting guide]*

'''''

_Clipboard keymaps defined in `lua/keymaps.lua`. Clipboard provider
configured in `lua/options.lua`. Full installation guide:
../guides/clipboard.md._

'''''

== Commenting (vim-commentary)

[width="100%",cols="34%,33%,33%",options="header",]
|===
|Keys |Mode |Action
|`gcc` |Normal |Toggle comment on current line

|`gc` |Visual |Toggle comment on selection

|`gc++<++motion++>++` |Normal |Toggle comment over a motion (e.g. `gcap`
= paragraph, `gc2j` = 3 lines)

|`:Commentary` |Command |Toggle comment on current line or range
(e.g. `:'++<++,'++>++Commentary`)
|===

`vim-commentary` uses the correct comment syntax for each filetype
automatically (`--` for Lua, `//` for JavaScript, `;;` for Lisp, `++#++`
for Python, etc.).

_Plugin configured in `lua/plugins/vim-commentary.lua`._

'''''

== Surround (vim-surround)

vim-surround adds keybindings to add, change, and delete surrounding
characters (quotes, brackets, tags, etc.) around text objects.

=== Change surroundings

[cols=",,",options="header",]
|===
|Keys |Action |Example
|`cs"'` |Change `"` to `'` |`"hello"` ŌåÆ `'hello'`

|`cs'++<++q++>++` |Change `'` to `++<++q++>++` tags |`'hello'` ŌåÆ
`++<++q++>++hello++<++/q++>++`

|`cst"` |Change tag to `"` |`++<++q++>++hello++<++/q++>++` ŌåÆ `"hello"`
|===

=== Delete surroundings

[cols=",,",options="header",]
|===
|Keys |Action |Example
|`ds"` |Delete surrounding `"` |`"hello"` ŌåÆ `hello`

|`dst` |Delete surrounding HTML tag |`++<++p++>++hello++<++/p++>++` ŌåÆ
`hello`
|===

=== Add surroundings

Cursor must be inside (or on) the text to surround. `ys` = "`you surround`".

[width="100%",cols="34%,33%,33%",options="header",]
|===
|Keys |Action |Example
|`ysiw"` |Surround word with `"` |`hello` ŌåÆ `"hello"`

|`ysiw(` |Surround word with `( )` (with spaces) |`hello` ŌåÆ `( hello )`

|`ysiw)` |Surround word with `()` (no spaces) |`hello` ŌåÆ `(hello)`

|`yss)` |Surround entire line with `()` |`hello world` ŌåÆ `(hello world)`

|`ysit++<++em++>++` |Surround inner tag content with `++<++em++>++`
|`++<++p++>++hello++<++/p++>++` ŌåÆ
`++<++p++><++em++>++hello++<++/em++><++/p++>++`
|===

=== Visual mode

Select text in visual mode, then press `S` followed by the surround character.

[cols=",",options="header",]
|===
|Keys |Action
|`S"` |Surround selection with `"`
|`S)` |Surround selection with `()`
|`S++<++p++>++` |Surround selection with `++<++p++><++/p++>++` tags
|===

_Plugin loaded via `lua/plugins/init.lua`. No custom configuration ŌĆö
default vim-surround keybindings apply._
